title: Empty volume exports
body:
### Issue Description

Volume exports are empty and no error message during exports.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

Not sure if there is any steps to reproduce this, since the export has been working for these specific container for a long time but suddenly one day the export is only 1Kb in size, other containers on the same host still produce correct volume exports.

The containers themself works and i can use the data within the containers without issue.

Running the following command which gives zero output to console:
podman volume export --output backup_c4.tar volume-c4-data

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:

Empty 1Kb files when they should at least hold a couple of Mb and more.

1.0K Nov 14 13:56 backup_c1.tar
4.8M Nov 14 13:56 backup_c2.tar
213M Nov 14 13:57 backup_c3.tar
1.0K Nov 14 13:57 backup_c4.tar

container export for c1 should be around 64Mb and c4 should be around 63Mb

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected:

Full volume exports usable for restoring when needed.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

I'm running on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS and 4.9.3 is the latest version available there.

Not sure what the issue can be but are open to testing some things if needed.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Any chance you can try with a newer Podman? 4.9.3 may be the latest available but it's closing in on a year old.

And can you add `--log-level=debug` to your `podman volume export` (specifically on the volume that fails) and provide the output?
A `podman inspect` of the volume would also help.

Hi, when it comes to versions i try to keep it to par with what ubuntu delivers in their LTS versions, but will consider this for the long run.

Debug log:
```
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called export.PersistentPreRunE(podman volume export --log-level=debug --output backup_c1.tar volume-c1-data) 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"

DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime ocijail initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime ocijail: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runj initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runj: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/crun"            
INFO[0000] Setting parallel job count to 13             
DEBU[0000] Exporting volume data from /***/storage/volumes/volume-c1-data/_data to backup_c1.tar 
DEBU[0000] creating tarball of /***/storage/volumes/volume-c1-data/_data 
DEBU[0000] wrote tarball file backup_c1.tar             
DEBU[0000] Called export.PersistentPostRunE(podman volume export --log-level=debug --output backup_c1.tar volume-c1-data) 
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

Inspect on volume:
```
[
     {
          "Name": "volume-c1-data",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/***/storage/volumes/volume-c1-data/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2024-06-11T08:29:25.31066643+02:00",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {},
          "UID": 1,
          "GID": 1,
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "LockNumber": 2
     }
]
```

OK. There's nothing at all unusual about the volume, and it seems like our archiver did choose the right directory (the filepath is correct for our named volume schema; I'm assuming that `/***/storage/volumes/volume-c1-data/_data` does contain the volume data?). There doesn't appear to be anything obvious that would prevent us from archiving it. Can you check the permissions on the directory's contents versus other volumes? I'd expect us to get actual error messages off such an error, but nothing else jumps out as a potential cause.

Yes, `/***/storage/volumes/volume-c1-data/_data` contains data since the containers can run and i could use the date within them.

I have migrated 2 of the `1Kb`exports containers to another podman server, they are purpose built development containers with SSH so they where easier to get the data out of, the third one is not so, there i'm a bit stuck on getting the data for migrating that container

> /***/storage/
```
drwx------ 3 165536 165536 4.0K Jun 11 08:29 vol-c1-data
drwx------ 3 232536 232536 4.0K Jun 11 09:40 vol-c2-data
drwx------ 3 299536 299536 4.0K Apr 30  2024 vol-c3-data
drwx------ 3 299536 299536 4.0K Apr 30  2024 vol-c4-data
```

> /***/storage/vol-c1-data/

Cannot see any files under `_data` on host side.
```
drwxr-xr-x 2 165536 165536 4.0K Apr 29  2024 _data
```

> /***/storage/vol-c2-data/

Can see files under `_data` on host side as root.
```
drwxr-xr-x 5 233537 233537 4.0K Jun 11 09:50 _data
```

> /***/storage/vol-c3-data/

Can see files under `_data` on host side as root.
```
drwxrwxrwt 6 300535 300535 4.0K Oct 26 10:31 _data
```

> /***/storage/vol-c4-data/

Cannot see any files under `_data` on host side as root.
```
drwxrwxrwx 2 299618 299618 124K Sep  2 18:40 _data
```

I see a difference in permissions and the ones that produce a correct export is `c2` and `c3`.

When the containers where created i used `--userns=auto:size=67000`(prepped on OS level first) and `:Z` permission settings for the `c1` & `c2` and `:rw` for `c3` & `c4`.

So yes a bit stumped on what could be the cause for it just to stop working, can it be a permission issue within the containers that is messing up the exports?

Update:
Checked the permissions within the containers on the data and it's looks the same for 2 of them that are running the same container image where one of them has a working export and the other one has not, the permissions are the same at least on the root folder.

Been investigating this a bit further and my conclusion is user error on my side.

One of the backup file that is 1Kb has actually no static data, it is session information for that system and somehow it has cleaned itself up to not use that folder anymore due to version changes from the container owner.

The other one is an error on my side, between 2 versions of my own built container i data location within the container making the already mounted volume pointing in the wrong place, this explains the 1Kb size on that one.

Sorry for taking up your time, closing this one as solved.
